# Operation: Doland Skeeter War

> A neighbor-to-neighbor field guide for knocking down West Nile-carrying mosquitoes in and around Doland, South Dakota. It teaches residents two proven defenses: eliminate standing water, and turn the water you keep into simple, wildlife-safe larval traps dosed with Bti (Mosquito Dunks and Bits). It is written in a plain-language military operation-order (OPORD) style.

## Pages

- [The Order (home)](https://dolandskeeterwar.com/): the full operation order — Situation (why West Nile matters locally), Mission, Execution (trap build plans for buckets, tubs, drums, stock tanks, tires, and improvised vessels), Sustainment (resupply and upkeep), and Command & Signal.
- [Printable flyer](https://dolandskeeterwar.com/doland-skeeter-war-flyer.html): a one-page printable summary for posting or handing out.

## Notes

- This is a free, volunteer, neighbor-to-neighbor effort — not an official program and not affiliated with any government agency.
- Guidance is general and educational. Always read and follow product labels; for West Nile Virus questions consult a clinician or the South Dakota Department of Health.
